name: G Pro X Superlight 2
tagline: The gold standard for competitive FPS gaming — ultra-light and flawless sensor.
description: The Logitech G Pro X Superlight 2 is the successor to one of the most acclaimed esports mice ever made. Weighing just 60 grams with the HERO 2 sensor delivering flawless tracking, it's the weapon of choice for countless professional players across CS2, Valorant, and Apex Legends. The Lightforce hybrid switches offer both the speed of optical and the satisfying feel of mechanical clicks.
pros: Premium PTFE feet for smooth glide
cons: Ambidextrous shape may not suit palm grip
verdict: If you're a competitive gamer who values precision and low weight above all else, the G Pro X Superlight 2 is the undisputed champion. It's the mouse that pros trust when tournaments are on the line.

name: DeathAdder V3 Pro
tagline: Legendary ergonomic shape refined for esports with cutting-edge technology.
description: The Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro takes the iconic ergonomic shape that millions of gamers love and strips it down to just 63 grams. With the Focus Pro 30K sensor and Gen-3 optical switches, it delivers instantaneous actuation and zero double-clicking issues. The shape is specifically sculpted for palm and claw grips, making long gaming sessions fatigue-free.
pros: Light for an ergonomic mouse at 63g
cons: Rubber grips can wear over time
Shape may feel narrow for wide grips
verdict: The DeathAdder V3 Pro is the ultimate ergonomic gaming mouse. If you use a palm or claw grip and want maximum comfort without sacrificing performance, this is your best bet at a competitive price.

name: MX Master 3S
tagline: The king of productivity mice — unmatched workflow features and ergonomics.
description: The Logitech MX Master 3S is the undisputed king of productivity mice. Its MagSpeed electromagnetic scroll wheel can switch between ratchet and free-spin modes automatically. With USB-C charging, multi-device support (up to 3 via Bluetooth + USB receiver), and the ability to work on virtually any surface including glass, it's the ultimate tool for creative professionals and power users.
pros: Works on any surface, even glass
Quiet click switches for office use
cons: Thumb button placement takes time to learn
verdict: If productivity is your priority, the MX Master 3S is simply the best mouse money can buy. The workflow features, build quality, and comfort are unmatched by any competitor.

name: Viper V3 Pro
tagline: Razer's lightest and fastest mouse ever — built for speed demons.
description: The Razer Viper V3 Pro pushes the boundaries of what's possible in a wireless gaming mouse. At just 54 grams, it's featherlight without resorting to honeycomb designs. The 8,000 Hz polling rate delivers input response eight times faster than standard mice, and the Focus Pro 30K sensor tracks flawlessly on any surface. It's the mouse that redefines speed.
cons: Low profile may not suit palm grip
Clicks can feel too light for some
verdict: The Viper V3 Pro is the fastest mouse on the planet. If you're a competitive player who wants every possible advantage in reaction time and speed, this is the mouse that delivers.

name: Pulsar X2V2
tagline: The community favorite — perfectly balanced shape at an insane weight.
description: The Pulsar X2V2 has taken the gaming community by storm with its meticulously refined symmetrical shape and ridiculously low weight of just 52 grams. The PAW3395 sensor is a proven performer, and the Kailh GM 8.0 switches deliver satisfying, crisp clicks. The included glass mouse feet offer unparalleled glide consistency, and the 100-hour battery means you'll barely remember to charge it.
cons: PAW3395 is good but not top-tier
Coating can feel slippery without grip tape
verdict: The Pulsar X2V2 offers the best value in the ultra-lightweight category. Its refined shape, glass feet, and incredible battery life make it a top recommendation for anyone looking to go light without going broke.

name: EC2-CW
tagline: Pure no-nonsense esports ergonomics — plug, play, and dominate.
description: The Zowie EC2-CW carries forward the legendary EC shape that has defined esports ergonomics for over a decade. There's no flashy RGB, no complex software — just a perfectly sculpted shape and reliable performance. The EC2 is the medium-sized variant ideal for claw and palm grip users. Professional CS2 players still swear by this shape because it simply works.
pros: Legendary ergonomic shape loved by pros
No software needed — plug and play
Large PTFE feet for smooth tracking
verdict: The EC2-CW is for purists who believe the best mouse is one you forget you're holding. If you want a proven esports shape without distractions, this is the mouse that thousands of pros have chosen time and again.

name: Xlite V3
tagline: The ergonomic featherweight — comfort meets ultralight performance.
description: The Pulsar Xlite V3 achieves what was once thought impossible — an ergonomic mouse under 50 grams. At 49g, it's lighter than most ambidextrous gaming mice while offering the comfort of a full ergonomic shape. The glass mouse feet provide an ultra-consistent glide, and the 100-hour battery life ensures it's always ready for action. It's the perfect mouse for palm grip gamers who want ultralight performance.
pros: Unbelievably light at 49g for an ergo shape
Solid build — no creaking or flex
cons: Small hands may find it too large
Software is basic compared to competitors
verdict: The Xlite V3 is a marvel of engineering. If you want an ergonomic shape but refuse to compromise on weight, this is the only mouse that truly delivers both at just 49 grams.

name: Orochi V2
tagline: The ultimate travel companion — tiny, versatile, and lasts forever.
description: The Razer Orochi V2 is the perfect mouse for people on the go. Its compact design fits easily into any laptop bag, and it supports both Bluetooth and 2.4GHz wireless. The real showstopper is battery life — up to 950 hours on a single AA battery. Despite its small size, it packs a capable sensor and comfortable shape that works for both casual gaming and productivity.
pros: Uses standard AA or AAA batteries
cons: Too small for large hands or palm grip
Click feel is not as refined
verdict: The Orochi V2 is the best travel mouse you can buy. Its dual wireless, insane battery life, and budget price make it a no-brainer for anyone who needs a reliable mouse on the go.

name: Anzu V2 Wireless
tagline: Indian gaming brand's ultra-lightweight champion — elite performance at budget.
description: The Kreo Anzu V2 Wireless is a breakthrough for the Indian gaming peripherals market. Weighing an astonishing 49 grams, it matches the weight of elite premium mice at a fraction of the cost. Tailored specifically for competitive FPS titles like CS2 and Valorant, its ergonomic shape provides a fatigue-free grip for extended sessions. The Huano switches offer crisp, tactile feedback with zero double-clicking risk.
cons: Battery life is moderate at 45 hrs
verdict: For Indian gamers looking to upgrade to a top-tier ultralight wireless mouse without breaking the bank, the Kreo Anzu V2 is an absolute steal.

name: Chimera V2 Wireless
tagline: The ultimate modular gaming mouse — interchangeable shells and hot-swap switches.
description: The Kreo Chimera V2 is a modular enthusiast's dream. It features a unique hot-swappable switch design, allowing users to swap switches (Omron, Kailh, Huano) without soldering. Additionally, it comes with interchangeable magnetic solid and honeycomb shells, letting you customize both the physical aesthetic and weight. Tri-mode connectivity (2.4GHz, Bluetooth 5.1, and USB-C wired) ensures compatibility with any setup.
cons: Slightly heavy compared to ultralight models
Honeycomb shell exposes interior to dust
verdict: If you love physical customization, modularity, and hot-swappable components, the Chimera V2 is the most unique and fun budget mouse you can buy.

name: Hawk Wired
tagline: Reliable budget wired performance — precision sensor and multiple buttons.
description: The Kreo Hawk Wired is designed for entry-level gamers who want a highly reliable, high-performance mouse without wireless latency or battery worries. Equipped with the PixArt PMW3327 sensor and 8 programmable buttons, it offers top-tier precision and configuration. The symmetrical shape fits a wide range of grip styles, and the software supports customizable RGB lighting profiles and complex macros.
cons: Large size not ideal for tiny hands
Cable drag without a mouse bungee
verdict: For budget-conscious gamers looking for a highly customizable and durable wired gaming mouse, the Kreo Hawk offers a fantastic performance-to-price ratio.

name: Harpy Lightweight
tagline: The featherweight entry-level wired speedster — just 55 grams.
description: The Kreo Harpy is one of the most affordable lightweight mice on the market. Weighing just 55 grams due to its honeycomb shell design, it's built for rapid flick shots and speed. It features customizable RGB, a paracord-like flexible cable, and an Instant A825 sensor that supports up to 12,800 DPI via software interpolation. It's the ultimate low-cost entry point into ultra-lightweight gaming.
pros: Very light at 55g for fast movements
cons: Entry-level sensor has low malfunction speed
Honeycomb design is susceptible to dust/moisture
Clicks can feel mushy over time
verdict: If you're on a tight budget but want to experience a 55g lightweight gaming mouse with high-speed gliding, the Kreo Harpy is a great option.

name: G502 X Plus
tagline: The evolution of the world's most popular gaming mouse — with LIGHTFORCE switches.
description: The Logitech G502 X Plus is the latest iteration of the legendary G502 shape. Stripped of weight down to 106 grams and packing the brand new LIGHTFORCE hybrid optical-mechanical switches, it delivers optical speed and reliability with satisfying mechanical tactility. Features active 8-zone RGB lighting, a dual-mode scroll wheel (infinite / ratchet), and a removable DPI-shift button.
pros: Iconic ergonomic shape with thumb rest
cons: Heavier than modern FPS mice at 106g
RGB lights turn off under palm to save power
verdict: If you love feature-rich, comfortable mice for general gaming and multi-genre titles, the G502 X Plus remains the gold standard of comfort and versatility.

name: G305 LightSpeed
tagline: The undisputed budget wireless king — compact shape, flawless sensor, and lasts months.
description: The Logitech G305 LightSpeed packs flagship gaming performance into a highly affordable package. Featuring the legendary egg-shaped design and a flawless HERO sensor, it has zero sensor delay or wireless lag. Using a single AA battery, it runs for an incredible 250 hours in performance mode, making it an insanely low-maintenance setup.
pros: Egg shape is highly comfortable for claw/fingertip
cons: 99g weight is slightly heavy (requires AAA mod)
verdict: The G305 is the best entry-level wireless gaming mouse ever made. It's affordable, reliable, and performs like a flagship model.

name: MX Anywhere 3S
tagline: The ultimate portable productivity mouse — quiet clicks, MagSpeed scroll, and glass tracking.
description: The Logitech MX Anywhere 3S is a compact wireless productivity mouse built for developers, creators, and travelers. Outfitted with an 8,000 DPI Darkfield sensor, it tracks flawlessly on any surface, including high-gloss glass. The MagSpeed electromagnetic scroll wheel offers rapid, silent scrolling (up to 1,000 lines per second) and switches between ratchet and free-spin modes automatically. Supports whisper-quiet click switches and Flow multi-device control.
pros: Highly portable and durable compact size
cons: Too small for palm grip or large hands
125 Hz polling rate is not for gaming
No horizontal scroll wheel (uses button combo)
Logi Bolt receiver not always included
verdict: If you need a highly precise, portable mouse for coffee shops, offices, and travel, the MX Anywhere 3S is the absolute best companion for your laptop.

name: Lift Vertical Ergonomic
tagline: Ergonomic comfort redefined for small-to-medium hands — handshake vertical design.
description: The Logitech Lift is an ergonomic vertical mouse designed to alleviate wrist pressure and muscle strain. Sculpted at a 57-degree angle, it places your hand in a natural handshake grip posture, relieving stress on your forearm. It features whisper-quiet clicks, a SmartWheel for seamless speed/precision scrolling, and multi-device connection for up to 3 computers. Specifically optimized for small-to-medium hand sizes.
pros: Relieves wrist strain and forearm pressure
Pair with up to 3 devices via Bluetooth
cons: Not suitable for large hand sizes (requires MX Vertical)
High learning curve for vertical grip
Heavy weight (125g) is not for gaming
verdict: If your wrist aches during long office hours and you have small-to-medium hands, the Logitech Lift is a life-changing investment in ergonomic comfort.

name: G102 LightSync
tagline: The undisputed entry-level wired champion — simple shape, reliable performance, and dynamic RGB.
description: The Logitech G102 LightSync (known as G203 in some regions) is the absolute favorite entry-level wired gaming mouse worldwide. Outfitted with a gaming-grade 8,000 DPI sensor and mechanical button tensioning, it delivers crisp, reliable clicks and flawless tracking. Features dynamic 3-zone LIGHTSYNC RGB customization to style your setup.
cons: Cable is rubberized and slightly stiff
Sensor is basic compared to HERO
Medium size not ideal for massive hands
Feet are basic PTFE and wear down fast
verdict: If you want an affordable, high-quality gaming mouse that performs exceptionally well in competitive games, the G102 is the best wired budget mouse on the market.

name: G703 LightSpeed
tagline: The esports ergonomic wireless icon — legendary shape, heavy-duty clicks, and HERO sensor.
description: The Logitech G703 LightSpeed is a legendary ergonomic wireless gaming mouse. Featuring Zowie-like ergo sculpting with rubberized side grips and a deep palm support bump, it is widely considered one of the most comfortable mice ever made. Equipped with the class-leading HERO 25K sensor and POWERPLAY compatibility for infinite wireless charging.
pros: Excellent rubber side grips for control
cons: Heavier than modern ultralight mice at 95g
Battery life of 60 hours is mediocre
Scroll wheel encoder issues can occur
verdict: For palm and claw grip gamers who want a wireless ergonomic mouse with substantial feel and esports-grade performance, the G703 remains an absolute masterpiece.

name: G Pro Wireless
tagline: The pioneer of lightweight wireless esports — modular side buttons and symmetrical shape.
description: The Logitech G Pro Wireless (GPW) revolutionized competitive esports. Stripped to 80 grams with a thin-wall shell design, it features removable modular side buttons on both the left and right, making it a true ambidextrous mouse. Outfitted with the HERO 25K sensor and lightspeed wireless for professional-grade latency.
pros: True ambidextrous design with modular buttons
Perfect safe shape fits almost any grip
Solid shell with zero flex or holes
cons: Side buttons can feel slightly mushy
verdict: The G Pro Wireless is a landmark mouse in gaming history. If you are left-handed or prefer modular side buttons, this remains the ultimate competitive choice.

name: Pebble 2 M350s
tagline: The ultimate minimalist pocket mouse — silent clicks, slim design, and multi-device Bluetooth.
description: The Logitech Pebble 2 M350s is an ultra-slim, lightweight, and silent portable mouse. Designed for students, digital nomads, and travel, its flat pebble-like profile easily fits into any pocket or laptop sleeve. Equipped with Silent Touch technology that reduces click noise by 90%, and supports seamless switching between 3 devices via Bluetooth.
pros: Bluetooth connectivity pairs with up to 3 devices
Very cheap and stylish color choices
cons: Flat shape has no ergonomic palm support
Not suitable for gaming or heavy work
Sensor does not track on glass
verdict: If you need a simple, silent, and ultra-portable mouse to throw in your backpack for casual laptop use, the Pebble 2 is unmatched in design and portability.

name: MX Ergo Wireless Trackball
tagline: The ultimate trackball for ergonomic relief — 20-degree adjustable tilt.
description: The Logitech MX Ergo is the flagship trackball mouse designed for maximum comfort. It features a unique adjustable hinge that allows you to tilt the mouse by 0 or 20 degrees, aligning your hand in a more natural position to reduce muscle strain. Since tracking is handled entirely via the thumb-operated trackball, the mouse remains stationary, making it perfect for cramped desks.
pros: Requires zero arm movement to control
Great for small workspaces or uneven surfaces
Solid construction with heavy premium base
cons: Trackball requires regular cleaning from dust
Not suitable for gaming or fast-paced actions
High learning curve for trackball movement
verdict: The MX Ergo is a lifesaver for users suffering from severe wrist pain, carpal tunnel, or repetitive strain injuries (RSI). It offers absolute physical comfort.

name: G903 LightSpeed
tagline: The flagship customization powerhouse — ambidextrous shape, infinite scroll, and 11 buttons.
description: The Logitech G903 LightSpeed is the ultimate multi-button gaming mouse. Outfitted with up to 11 programmable buttons and modular side clickers, it fits both left and right-handed players perfectly. Features a dual-mode hyper-fast scroll wheel (infinite scroll), class-leading HERO 25K sensor, and compatibility with POWERPLAY for perpetual wireless charging.
pros: True ambidextrous layout with modular buttons
cons: Heavy compared to FPS mice at 110g
Complex shape has many crevices for dirt
verdict: If you want a premium, high-customization mouse with infinite scrolling, modular side buttons, and esports-grade tracking, the G903 is an outstanding flagship choice.

name: Basilisk V3
tagline: The ergonomic customization titan — HyperScroll tilt wheel and 11 programmable buttons.
description: The Razer Basilisk V3 is a highly customizable wired gaming mouse designed for maximum comfort and utility. Direct competitor to the Logitech G502 series, it features a unique HyperScroll tilt wheel that can automatically toggle between tactile and free-spinning scroll modes. Outfitted with 11 programmable buttons, a dedicated thumb rest, and 11 Chroma RGB lighting zones for absolute visual flair.
pros: Ergonomic palm-rest shape is highly comfortable
cons: Software is heavy on system resources
verdict: If you want a comfortable, feature-packed wired mouse for general gaming and office work, the Basilisk V3 is one of the best models ever built.

name: Cobra Wired
tagline: Ultra-lightweight compact gaming — crisp optical switches and beautiful Chroma underglow.
description: The Razer Cobra is an ultra-lightweight wired gaming mouse designed for smaller hands. At just 58 grams, it features a sleek symmetrical shape (successor to the Viper Mini) that claw and fingertip grip users love. Equipped with Gen-3 optical switches and a highly flexible Speedflex cable for drag-free glide. Features customizable Chroma RGB underglow.
pros: Gen-3 optical switches are fast and durable
cons: Too small for large hands or palm grip
Plastic coating can feel slightly cheap
verdict: The Razer Cobra is the perfect successor to the legendary Viper Mini. It's a lightweight, reliable, and affordable gaming weapon.

name: Naga V2 Pro
tagline: The king of MMO/MOBA gaming — 3 swappable side plates and customizable scroll wheel.
description: The Razer Naga V2 Pro is the ultimate wireless MMO gaming mouse. It features a unique modular design with 3 interchangeable side plates (2-button, 6-button, and 12-button layouts) to customize controls for any genre. Outfitted with the Focus Pro 30K sensor, Gen-3 optical switches, and the HyperScroll Pro wheel, which lets users customize scroll resistance and tactile force electronically.
cons: Bulky shape is not for small hands
Side plates can feel slightly loose over time
verdict: For serious MMO, MOBA, or macro-heavy players, the Naga V2 Pro has absolutely no equal. It is the most customizable mouse in existence.

name: DeathAdder Essential
tagline: The classic ergonomic legacy made budget-friendly — pure comfort and reliability.
description: The Razer DeathAdder Essential is the entry-level version of the world's most famous ergonomic gaming mouse. It retains the classic comfortable DeathAdder shape that has dominated esports for over a decade. Outfitted with a gaming-grade 6,400 DPI sensor, 5 programmable Hyperesponse buttons, and single-zone green LED lighting, it offers no-nonsense comfort.
pros: Great for large hands and palm grip
cons: Sensor is entry-level and has low DPI
verdict: If you want the legendary ergonomic comfort of a DeathAdder at the lowest possible price, the DeathAdder Essential is a highly reliable choice.

name: Viper V2 Pro
tagline: Esports champion wireless speedster — ultra-light 58g and Focus Pro 30K sensor.
description: The Razer Viper V2 Pro is a high-performance wireless esports mouse. Weighing just 58 grams, it strips down features from the previous Viper Ultimate to deliver absolute speed and precision. Outfitted with the Focus Pro 30K optical sensor, Gen-3 optical switches, and compatibility with Razer's 4,000 Hz HyperPolling dongle. The low-profile symmetrical shape is optimized for claw and fingertip grip pros.
pros: Optical switches have zero double-click issues
cons: Low profile shape is not for palm grip
verdict: The Viper V2 Pro is a competitive gaming masterpiece. For fingertip and claw grip FPS players who want elite wireless tracking, this is a top-tier weapon.

name: Pro Click Wireless
tagline: Premium ergonomic office mouse designed with Humanscale — perfect posture and workflow.
description: The Razer Pro Click is a premium wireless productivity mouse designed in collaboration with Humanscale, the world's leading expert in office ergonomics. Sculpted with a tilted angle to keep the wrist neutral, it prevents wrist pain and forearm tension. Features multi-host connectivity for up to 4 devices, a micro-scroll wheel, 8 programmable buttons, and up to 400 hours of battery life.
pros: Collaborative design with Humanscale for maximum comfort
Stylish white and gray premium office aesthetic
cons: Uses older micro-USB port for charging
Low polling rate on Bluetooth mode
Relatively large size not for travel
verdict: The Razer Pro Click is a perfect fusion of professional ergonomics and gaming-grade precision. It is the best ergonomic office mouse for developers and designers who spend hours at a desk.
